summary: DHH criticizes the tendency of programming language advocates to seize on any hint of weakness in a competing language as an opportunity for self-congratulation. An unverified internal memo about Java issues specific to Solaris sparked PHP advocates to make grandiose claims about open source languages working perfectly everywhere. DHH calls out this jingoistic cheerleading as dishonest and counterproductive, noting that all languages have flaws. He argues that presenting any language as perfect undermines credibility.
key_points: Language community advocates pounce on any hint of competitor weakness like hyenas sensing blood
The original Java critique was narrow and platform-specific, but was treated as a general indictment
Nationalistic cheerleading for programming languages is dishonest — all platforms have issues
Claiming any language is perfect undermines your own credibility
money_quotes: It's a hint of a possible drop of speculative blood, and a hint is all it takes. The language hyaenas stand ready.
Oh puh-lease. It's "our men and women", it's flags caught in the wind, it's a national song, and it's sickening.
It undermines your credibility to present any language as Perfect. Please stop.
key_insight: Programming language tribalism that exploits competitors' minor issues to make grandiose claims of perfection is dishonest and self-defeating.
